description: Producer DID resolution is an SSRF vector identical to cross-registry resolution. A registry verifying a publish request (RFC-ACDP-0003 §2.1 step 6), and a consumer verifying a retrieved context end-to-end, both dereference the producer-controlled did:web identifier into an HTTPS URL. A did:web authority resolving to a loopback address (127.0.0.0/8, ::1, or the unspecified address 0.0.0.0) MUST be refused by default — see RFC-ACDP-0008 §4.8. A WebResolver that has no IP-range restriction can be turned into an SSRF proxy against the registry's own process-internal listeners.
ssrf_rule: RFC-ACDP-0008 §4.8 (Producer DID resolution SSRF protection — loopback)

input.scenario: A publish request whose body.agent_id is a did:web identifier resolving to a loopback address

expected.behavior: The resolver MUST refuse before connecting. Both IPv4 (127.0.0.0/8) and IPv6 (::1) loopback MUST be filtered, and 0.0.0.0 MUST also be rejected. The refusal is permanent, policy-driven, and producer-caused — key_resolution_failed (HTTP 400, not retryable), NOT key_resolution_unreachable (HTTP 502, retryable).
expected.test_mode_exception: A test harness MAY permit loopback did:web targets ONLY when an explicit non-default test-mode SSRF policy is configured. This MUST NOT be the default.
expected.rationale: Loopback resolution targets the registry's own host, exposing process-internal HTTP listeners (admin APIs, metrics, debug endpoints) normally bound only to 127.0.0.1.
